headline: Malay Reservation Land Covers 23% of Peninsular Malaysia
context: Malay Reservation Enactments across Peninsular states designate approximately 23% of total land area as Malay Reservation, restricting transfer and development to Malay owners only.

cards.big: 23% of Peninsular Malaysia is classified as Malay Reservation Land.
cards.sub: This land cannot be sold to, leased to, or developed by non-Malays under state enactments.

cards.big: Malay Reservation Land is valued at 30-40% below equivalent freehold land.
cards.sub: Restricted marketability depresses value. Malay landowners hold an estimated RM180B in discounted assets.

cards.big: The enactments date to 1913 — designed by colonial authorities.
cards.sub: Their stated purpose was protecting Malay peasants from predatory land purchases. The context has fundamentally changed.

cards.big: Bank Negara allows only 60% loan-to-value on Malay Reservation Land.
cards.sub: This limits Malay owners' ability to leverage their largest asset for business capital.

cards.big: Land reserved for Malay ownership that Malay owners cannot fully monetise. Protection has become a cage.
Malay Reservation Land reform is politically sensitive but economically urgent. Unlocking RM180B in depressed land value would benefit Malay owners more than maintaining the restriction.
